plot_frame_box.description: Pixel bounding box of the chart data extent (1985..2025 along x, 0%..100% along y). x-axis tick line at y=511; gridlines at y={127,204,281,358,435,511}. Top/bottom row map directly to data extremes.

x_axis.note: Tick labels for 1985 and 2025 are visually inset (centers at px=69 and px=696) relative to the linear interior-tick fit (1985→px=54.36, 2025→px=710.79). The interior tick spacing is exactly 82 px per 5 years (16.41 px/year); use that fit, not the inset 1985/2025 labels.

y_axis.note: Single linear fit covers 0%-100%. Detected gridline pixel spacing is 77 px per 20 pp uniformly (~3.84 px per percentage point). Although the user noted a broken axis (0-50% lower band, 80-100% upper band with a gap), inspection of the source image shows ticks 100/80/60/40/20/0 at evenly spaced pixel rows (127, 204, 281, 358, 435, 511) with the SAME 77 px spacing between consecutive ticks. The y-axis is continuous and linear; the visual whitespace between 60% and 80% is simply empty data space (no series falls in 60-80% over 1985-2025), not a broken axis. Both y_axis_lower and y_axis_upper blocks below are recorded to honor the chart-specific note, but they share identical m/b coefficients.

y_axis_lower.note: Lower band as if broken-axis; identical calibration to upper band because the axis is in fact continuous and linear.

y_axis_upper.note: Upper band as if broken-axis; identical calibration to lower band because the axis is in fact continuous and linear.

worked_example.scenario: Sweden's 2020 value of 98.49% should land in the top-right region of the upper band.
worked_example.verification: (629, 133) sits just below the 100% gridline (y=127) and at the 2020 x-tick column — matching Sweden's near-top trajectory in the upper band of the source image.

detection_internals.interior_year_fit_rule: Use 1990..2020 (inclusive) to fit year-vs-pixel; 1985 and 2025 labels are inset from the data extent and must NOT be used for calibration.
detection_internals.broken_axis_check: Verified the axis is single-linear: spacing 100%→80%=77 px, 80%→60%=77 px, 60%→40%=77 px, 40%→20%=77 px, 20%→0%=76 px. Uniform => continuous axis.

occlusion_caveats.United States: Dark blue US line is overlapped by UK (dark red) in 1993-1994 and by World (magenta) in 2012-2014. Those 5 points (1993, 1994, 2012, 2013, 2014) are linearly interpolated between neighbours and are flagged in interpolated_points.json.
occlusion_caveats.Germany: Germany data begins at 1986 (no marker at 1985 in source). 1985 is left empty (not interpolated).
